feed.title: Google Cloud: Cloud Deploy
feed.subtitle: Changelog for Google Cloud product: Cloud Deploy

entry.content: Cloud Deploy is now available in the following region: asia-southeast3 (Bangkok)
entry.summary: Cloud Deploy is now available in the asia-southeast3 (Bangkok) region. This expansion brings our deployment capabilities closer to users in Southeast Asia, enabling faster and more reliable application deployments.
entry.title: Feature: Cloud Deploy now in Bangkok

entry.content: You can now deploy containerized workloads to Cloud Run worker pools (in Preview).
entry.summary: You can now deploy containerized workloads to Cloud Run worker pools. This new feature is currently in Preview and allows for more flexible deployment options.
entry.title: Feature: Deploy containers to Cloud Run worker pools

entry.content: The limit on deployment minutes per delivery pipeline has been removed. The only quota now enforced in Cloud Deploy is the system limit of 18,000 API requests per minute per region. Learn more.
entry.summary: The previous limit on deployment minutes per delivery pipeline has been removed. The only remaining quota in Cloud Deploy is the system limit of 18,000 API requests per minute per region. This change simplifies quota management for users.
entry.title: Change: Deployment minute limit removed

entry.content: We now upgrade the tools used for deployments more frequently. This includes tools like Skaffold, Kubectl, and Helm. You can also select specific versions of these tools when you create a release.
entry.summary: We've updated the deployment tools used in Cloud Deploy, including Skaffold, Kubectl, and Helm, to more recent versions. You can now also select specific tool versions when creating a release, giving you greater control over your deployments.
entry.title: Change: Updated deployment tools available

entry.content: You can now use custom constraints with Organization Policy to provide more granular control over specific fields for some Cloud Deploy resources. For more information, see Use custom organization policies.
entry.summary: Organization Policy now supports custom constraints for more granular control over Cloud Deploy resources.
entry.title: Feature: Custom constraints in Cloud Deploy
